response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong individual contributor with genuine backend and distributed-systems depth, but he is categorically unqualified for this CTO role. In over 15 years of engineering work there is no evidence of managing, hiring, or scaling any team — the single most disqualifying gap for an executive leadership position. His agentic AI experience is nascent and tooling-level only, beginning in April 2025 at Stripe with MCP code generators; this falls far short of the demonstrated production multi-agent systems the role requires. His fintech exposure is limited to one year as a senior developer at a small German investment platform, with no experience in credit systems, real-time risk, payment flows, or regulatory compliance infrastructure.
strengths: Deep high-throughput backend experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of real-time HTTP requests per second with low-latency async architecture
Current hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at Stripe, demonstrating awareness of the emerging tooling landscape
Broad polyglot technical range across TypeScript, Node.js, D, C++, mobile, and cloud infrastructure with a consistent pattern of delivering production systems
concerns: Zero engineering leadership or people-management experience anywhere in a 15+ year career — no team leads, no hires, no org scaling, no mention of direct reports

Agentic AI experience is recent, shallow, and tooling-level only; no evidence of production multi-agent systems, LLM evaluation methodology, observability pipelines, or reliability engineering at scale
No fintech infrastructure depth whatsoever — credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk modeling, margin optimization, and regulatory compliance are entirely absent from the resume
